case_name: EPX (A Child) v Milton Keynes University Hospital NHS Trust [2019] EWHC 1508 (QB) (14 June 2019)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The Claimant was born prematurely in a hospital in Great Yarmouth on 28 July 2015. She was transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit as a result of her prematurity and low gestational weight.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Her mother subsequently tested positive for Group B Streptococcus ('GBS'). The Claimant was given intravenous antibiotics because of her mother's GBS.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She deteriorated at home on 21/22 August 2005 and was brought back to hospital on the morning of 22 August 2005. She was ill and getting worse.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Despite intravenous antibiotics being commenced, she deteriorated further and was transferred to the Oxford Radcliffe hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: There it was later confirmed that she suffered very extensive brain injury as a result of GBS meningitis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claim was eventually settled for £1.837 million and annual periodical payments for care and case management in the sum of £204,000 per annum from December 2016 until December 2023, increasing to £230,504 per annum from December 2024 onwards.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Life expectancy was agreed by the parties to the age of 24 years.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Deterioration at home, brought to hospital
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: The Claimant was born prematurely and was initially doing well after discharge but deteriorated at home on 21/22 August 2005.
medications: Intravenous antibiotics given initially due to mother's GBS status

focused_exam.general: Patient appeared ill and was getting worse

requestables.name: Blood Cultures
requestables.description: Blood cultures to identify bacterial infection

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt immediate recall and treatment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to hospital with deterioration

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Blood cultures taken and results received

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with signs of infection
decision_points.prompt: What should be done after receiving positive blood culture results?

options.description: Recall patient to hospital for immediate treatment
options.clinical_reasoning: Immediate treatment required for confirmed GBS infection
Failure to act on critical lab result

actual_action_defendant.description: No action was taken after positive blood culture result

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been recalled for treatment

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to act on positive blood culture led to deterioration
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been recalled for immediate treatment

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered extensive brain injury due to GBS meningitis

key_findings: Failure to act on positive blood culture
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented brain injury

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to recall patient after positive blood culture
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate treatment could have prevented brain injury

allegations.allegation: Failure to act on positive blood culture
